title: Full-Time Clinical Psychologist
description:
We are seeking a qualified and experienced Clinical Psychologist to join our growing team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in delivering evidence-based treatments, particularly Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and experience in administering neuropsychological assessments for youths, adolescents, and adults, as well as strong report writing skills.

  • Develop and implement individualised treatment plans using evidence-based interventions, with a primary focus on CBT.

  • Provide individual, couples, group therapy sessions for clients, both in-person and virtually

  • Conduct neuro-psychological assessments including ADHD, learning needs, dyslexia, and other related conditions.

  • Participate in team meetings and contribute to ongoing service development and improvement initiatives.

  • Collaborate with our interdisciplinary team to develop and implement client-centered treatment plans.

  • Work closely with our 24/7 helpline responder team to manage risk, ensure the safety of clients in crisis situations and provide crisis interventions

  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date client records in accordance with professional and legal standards.

  • Continuously update professional knowledge and skills through participation in continuing education and professional development activities.

  • Ability to commit to clinic operating hours of Monday to Friday 10-9pm and Saturday 10-2pm on a roster basis. There will be 2 evening working days (1-9pm) each week and 2 working Saturdays each month. 

Requirements:

  • A Master's Degree in Clinical Psychology 

  • At least 3 years of post-graduate clinical experience, preferably in a mental health setting.

  • Expertise in individual and group facilitation

  • Proficient in planning and executing online seminars with the use of various web platforms and adept at creating engaging content.

  • Proficient in effectively navigating and managing crisis situations.

  • Experience in administering psychological assessments

  • Strong knowledge and experience in delivering evidence-based treatments with a focus on CBT as a core treatment modality.

  • Current registration or eligibility for registration as a Clinical Psychologist (Singapore Psychological Society or SPS)

  • Must be able to conduct in-person sessions at Intellect's office location or our partnered client's location.

  • Good to have: Experience in providing clinical supervision to other mental health professionals.

Key Attributes:

  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills.

  • Empathetic and compassionate approach to client care.

  • Strong collaboration skills and the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team.

  • Commitment to evidence-based practice and ongoing professional development.

  • Adaptability and flexibility in working with diverse client populations and presenting issues.

  • High level of organization and time management skills.

    To help us process applications more quickly, please submit yours in this link: https://apply.workable.com/intellecthq/j/2A99A49B37/apply/

postedCompany.description:
Intellect (intellect.co) is a venture-backed digital health company reinventing how mental health is in Asia. Our goal is to make it simple, easy and stigma-free for everyone.

We're building an entirely new way for modern organisations to easily provide support for their employees’ mental health & mental wellbeing, no matter where they are on their journey.


Join us on our mission to eliminate barriers to mental health and to create a wellbeing approach that people love using.
postedCompany.name: INTELLECT COMPANY PTE. LTD.
